channel.title: IBD LitMonitor
channel.description: Daily IBD literature monitoring - organized by clinical subfield

item.title: Human gut flagellome profiling using FlaPro reveals TLR5-related phenotype-specific alterations in IBD.
item.description: Gut microbes - Flagellin, the structural protein of bacterial flagella, activates the innate immune receptor Toll-like receptor 5 (TLR5). However, the ability of different flagellins to bind and stimulate TLR5 va...

item.title: AI-Powered Discovery of Rosmarinic Acid as a Novel Ferroptosis Inhibitor for Ulcerative Colitis via Targeting the ALOX15-VDAC1 Axis.
item.description: Research (Washington, D.C.) - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease with limited mechanism-based therapies, and ferroptosis-an iron-dependent lipid peroxidation-driven cell death-has emerged as a criti...

item.title: Enterococcus hirae Y-HS Alleviates Ulcerative Colitis by Activating PXR/Nrf2-mediated Metabolic-immune Crosstalk.
item.description: Probiotics and antimicrobial proteins - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease with limited non-invasive biomarkers and variable responses to probiotics. This study investigates the probiotic potential of Enteroc...

item.title: The NLRP3 inflammasome as a dynamic context-dependent regulator: mechanisms, disease progression, and therapeutic implications.
item.description: Journal of translational medicine - Review - The NLRP3 inflammasome is a central regulator of innate immunity and inflammation, but its functions extend beyond a simple pro-inflammatory role. Increasing evidence indicates that the biological ...

item.title: Traditional Chinese Medicine in the Treatment of Ulcerative Colitis: From Pharmacokinetics to Personalized Therapy.
item.description: The American journal of Chinese medicine - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease driven by immune dysregulation, intestinal microbiota imbalance, and disruption of the mucosal barrier. Current clinical management i...

item.title: Integrative metagenomics and structural bioinformatics identify explainable gut microbial variants associated with Crohn's disease.
item.description: PloS one - Metagenomics has revealed disease-associated shifts in microbial taxa and functions in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) patients. However, the role of genomic variation in gut commensals remains po...

item.title: From copper imbalance to immunometabolic remodeling: cuproptosis-related vulnerability and therapeutic hypotheses in autoimmune diseases.
item.description: Inflammation research : official journal of the European Histamine Research Society ... [et al.] - Review - Autoimmune diseases (AIDs) are characterized by disrupted immune tolerance, chronic inflammation, and organ-specific tissue injury. Their pathogenesis involves the interplay between genetic suscept...

item.title: SHIP1 is required for T cell surveillance of occult malignancies.
item.description: Cellular and molecular life sciences : CMLS - SHIP1-deficient mice develop severe, transmural Crohn's-like ileitis and die prematurely. Recent analysis of a human Crohn's disease (CD) cohort revealed a significant subset of patients have sever...

item.title: Inflammasomes in Intestinal Inflammation: Guardians of Mucosal Homeostasis, Amplifiers of Disease, and Targets for Intervention.
item.description: Internal medicine (Tokyo, Japan) - The intestinal mucosa is continuously exposed to dietary antigens, commensal microorganisms, and enteric pathogens, thereby depending on innate immune pathways that detect danger without compromisi...

item.title: Vitamin D3 facilitates mucosal healing and modulates macrophage polarization in DSS-induced colitis, accompanied by restoration of epithelial Hippo-YAP signaling.
item.description: European journal of pharmacology - Damage to the epithelial barrier and dysregulated immune responses are core features of ulcerative colitis (UC). Although vitamin D3 (VD3) is widely recognized for immunomodulatory actions, how it ...

item.title: Patient preferences toward the attributes of treatment used for inflammatory bowel disease in Saudi Arabia: Subgroup analysis from an international multicenter discrete choice experiment.
item.description: Saudi journal of gastroenterology : official journal of the Saudi Gastroenterology Association - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD), including Crohn's disease (CD), and ulcerative colitis (UC), are chronic autoimmune disorders with increasing prevalence in Saudi Arabia (KSA). Understanding pati...

item.title: Optimizing bowel preparation regimens for colon capsule endoscopy: an umbrella review (overview of systematic reviews).
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastrointestinal endoscopy - Colon capsule endoscopy (CCE) is a minimally invasive alternative to colonoscopy, but its diagnostic performance depends entirely on adequate mucosal cleansing (adequate cleansing rate, ACR) and co...

item.title: A Longitudinal Disease Progression Model Characterizing Endoscopic Response and Remission in Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Journal of pharmacokinetics and pharmacodynamics - RCT - Crohn's disease (CD) is a heterogeneous, progressive inflammatory bowel disease in which substantial placebo effects often confound assessment of therapeutic efficacy. A placebo-based disease model...

item.title: Acneiform Eruption with Deucravacitinib: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
item.description: Clinical and experimental dermatology - Deucravacitinib, an oral selective tyrosine kinase 2 (TYK2) inhibitor, is an effective treatment for chronic plaque psoriasis. Its use is widening to treat other autoinflammatory conditions, but ac...

item.title: Racial and Ethnic Patterns Observed in Inflammatory Bowel Disease Prevalence, Clinical Burden, and Healthcare Resource Utilization in the United States: Findings from an Administrative Claims Database.
item.description: Advances in therapy - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic, immune-mediated gastrointestinal disorder comprised of Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC). Real-world data on racial differences in dise...

item.title: Epidemiology of primary sclerosing cholangitis in general and IBD populations: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
item.description: Hepatology international - Primary Sclerosing Cholangitis (PSC) is a chronic cholestatic liver condition that is closely associated with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). PSC is associated with substantial long-term hepatobi...

item.title: Short, not skinny: The distinct sarcopenia phenotype of pediatric primary sclerosing cholangitis-inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - Sarcopenia is prevalent in pediatric liver disease and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) and predicts adverse outcomes. We aimed to compare sarcopenia prevalence in pediatric primary sclerosing chol...

item.title: Primary sclerosing cholangitis displays distinct colonic mucosa topography yet a shared mast cell state with ulcerative colitis.
item.description: Nature communications - Primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) is a chronic, progressing cholestatic disease that often co-occurs with inflammatory bowel disease (PSC-IBD). PSC-IBD affecting the colon (PSC-ulcerative coliti...

item.title: Integration of genetic evidence to identify approved drug targets.
item.description: Genome medicine - Drugs targeting genes supported by human genetic evidence are more likely to succeed in clinical trials. While previous approaches have benchmarked individual methods such as genome-wide associatio...

item.title: A genetic variant of adenylate cyclase 7 associated with ulcerative colitis shows impaired function and G-protein-coupled receptor signaling.
item.description: Human genetics - A missense variant of adenylate cyclase 7 (AC7), p.Asp439Glu, has been significantly associated with ulcerative colitis (UC) in genome-wide association studies. Previous work suggested that this va...
